steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: “the Puerto Rican municipality of Manatí is well known for its history as a cultural center in the early 20th [blank]” and “this distinction has earned it the fitting nickname of “the Athens of Puerto Rico.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses, making D correct. Choices A, B, and C violate sentence boundary rules: A omits the required comma before the coordinating conjunction, B leaves two independent clauses unpunctuated, and C uses only a comma without a conjunction. That matches choice D — D is correct.
